segments.text: So they brought the ark of God and placed it inside the tent that David had pitched for it. And they presented burnt offerings and peace offerings before God.
When David had finished sacrificing the burnt offerings and peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the LORD.
Then he distributed to every man and woman of Israel a loaf of bread, a date cake, and a raisin cake.
David appointed some of the Levites to minister before the ark of the LORD, to celebrate, to give thanks, and to praise the LORD, the God of Israel.
Asaph was the chief, Zechariah was second, then Jeiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel. They were to play the harps and lyres, while Asaph sounded the cymbals
and the priests Benaiah and Jahaziel blew the trumpets regularly before the ark of the covenant of God.
On that day David first committed to Asaph and his brothers this song of thanksgiving to the LORD:
Give thanks to the LORD; call upon His name; make known His deeds among the nations.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Sing to Him, sing praises to Him; tell of all His wonders.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Glory in His holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the LORD rejoice.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Seek out the LORD and His strength; seek His face always.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Remember the wonders He has done, His marvels, and the judgments He has pronounced,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O offspring of His servant Israel, O sons of Jacob, His chosen ones.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He is the LORD our God; His judgments carry throughout the earth.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Remember His covenant forever, the word He ordained for a thousand generations—
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: the covenant He made with Abraham, and the oath He swore to Isaac.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He confirmed it to Jacob as a decree, to Israel as an everlasting covenant:
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: ‘I will give you the land of Canaanas the portion of your inheritance.’
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When they were few in number, few indeed, and strangers in the land,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: they wandered from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He let no man oppress them; He rebuked kings on their behalf:
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: ‘Do not touch My anointed ones! Do no harm to My prophets!’
segments.speaker: David

text: Sing to the LORD, All the Earth

segments.text: Sing to the LORD, all the earth. Proclaim His salvation day after day.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Declare His glory among the nations, His wonderful deeds among all peoples.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised; He is to be feared above all gods.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For all the gods of the nations are idols, but it is the LORD who made the heavens.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Splendor and majesty are before Him; strength and joy fill His dwelling.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Ascribe to the LORD, O families of the nations, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Ascribe to the LORD the glory due His name; bring an offering and come before Him. Worship the LORD in the splendor of His holiness;
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: tremble before Him, all the earth. The world is firmly established; it cannot be moved.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let the heavens be glad, and the earth rejoice. Let them say among the nations,‘The LORD reigns!’
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let the sea resound, and all that fills it; let the fields exult, and all that is in them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Then the trees of the forest will sing for joy before the LORD, for He is coming to judge the earth.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good; His loving devotion endures forever.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Then cry out: ‘Save us, O God of our salvation; gather and deliver us from the nations, that we may give thanks to Your holy name, that we may glory in Your praise.’
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Then all the people said,
Amen!
segments.speaker: all the people

segments.text: and
Praise the LORD!
segments.speaker: all the people

segments.text: So David left Asaph and his brothers there before the ark of the covenant of the LORD, to minister there regularly according to the daily requirements,
along with Obed-edom and his sixty-eight relatives. Obed-edom son of Jeduthun, and also Hosah, were to be gatekeepers.
And David left Zadok the priest and his fellow priests before the tabernacle of the LORD at the high place in Gibeon
to regularly present burnt offerings to the LORD on the altar of burnt offerings, morning and evening, according to all that was written in the Law of the LORD, which He had commanded Israel to keep.
With them were Heman, Jeduthun, and the rest of those chosen and designated by name to give thanks to the LORD, for
His loving devotion endures forever.
Heman and Jeduthun had with them trumpets and cymbals for the music and instruments for the songs of God. And the sons of Jeduthun were stationed at the gate.
Then all the people departed for their homes, and David returned home to bless his household.
